source.title: CSB Safety Spotlight: Implementation of a Safety Management System is Key to a Safer Chemical Industry

context.operator: Airgas, Inc., an Air Liquide Company

actions_taken: Developed and implemented Industrial Risk Management Program
Created project design authority within the company
Applied more appropriate transfer pumps for nitrous oxide service
Upgraded maintenance requirements for flame arrestors following technical review
Updated procedures and training for drivers and operators during transfer operations
Issued personal gas monitors to personnel
Initiated literature reviews and third-party engineering assessments
Increased lessons learned and good safety practices sharing internally and with compressed gas industry
event.summary: On August 28, 2016, a nitrous oxide trailer truck exploded at the Airgas manufacturing facility in Cantonment, Florida. The explosion fatally injured the only Airgas employee on site and heavily damaged the facility. The most probable cause was that, during initial loading of a trailer truck, a pump heated nitrous oxide above its safe operating limits, initiating a decomposition reaction that propagated from the pump into the trailer truck and caused the explosion. The CSB investigation found that Airgas lacked an effective safety management system to identify, evaluate, and control process safety-related hazards. Following the incident, Airgas undertook a comprehensive initiative covering 17 areas of process safety improvement and rapidly implemented PSM components for its nitrous oxide operations.
recommendations: CSB issued a single recommendation to Airgas to complete its in-progress post-incident actions and continue safety initiatives for its nitrous oxide operations
Develop and implement PSM components including hazard analysis, management of change, and inherently safer design
Share lessons learned and good safety practices with the broader compressed gas industry
key_phrases: pump heated nitrous oxide above its safe operating limits
did not have an effective safety management system
17 different areas for process safety improvements

hazards.name: Nitrous oxide under pressure in transfer/storage system with decomposition potential

threats.name: Pump overheating nitrous oxide above safe operating limits during trailer loading
Absence of effective process safety management system

consequences.name: Nitrous oxide decomposition reaction propagating into trailer truck

controls.name: Appropriate transfer pumps for nitrous oxide service
controls.barrier_role: Prevent pump from overheating nitrous oxide above safe operating limits
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent thermal exceedance in pump

supporting_text: a pump heated nitrous oxide above its safe operating limits
applying more appropriate transfer pumps for nitrous oxide service

controls.name: Flame arrestors on nitrous oxide transfer system
controls.barrier_role: Prevent propagation of decomposition reaction from pump to trailer
controls.lod_basis: Secondary engineering barrier to arrest flame/decomposition propagation

supporting_text: upgrading maintenance requirements for flame arrestors (this followed a significant technical review of existing flame arrestor technology)
decomposition reaction that propagated from the pump into the trailer truck, causing the explosion

controls.name: Process Safety Management System (hazard identification, evaluation, and control)
controls.barrier_role: Identify, evaluate, and control process safety-related hazards before incident occurs
controls.lod_basis: Foundational administrative barrier for hazard management

supporting_text: Airgas did not have an effective safety management system that identifies, evaluates, and controls process safety-related hazards like those that led to the explosion

controls.name: Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) methodology
controls.barrier_role: Systematically identify and evaluate process hazards in nitrous oxide operations
controls.lod_basis: Structured hazard identification and risk assessment process

supporting_text: Airgas developed and implemented recommended PSM components for its nitrous oxide business by creating a new Industrial Risk Management Program, a process hazard analysis methodology

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) procedure
controls.barrier_role: Control changes to process equipment and operations to prevent introduction of new hazards
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to manage process and equipment changes safely

controls.name: Operator and driver procedures and training for transfer operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel follow safe operating practices during nitrous oxide transfer
controls.lod_basis: Human performance barrier through training and procedural compliance

supporting_text: updating procedures and training for drivers and operators during transfer operations

controls.name: Personal gas monitors for nitrous oxide exposure detection
controls.barrier_role: Detect nitrous oxide exposure and alert personnel to mitigate harm
controls.lod_basis: Detection and warning device to protect personnel from gas exposure

supporting_text: issuing personal gas monitors to personnel to help eliminate or mitigate potential exposure to nitrous oxide

controls.name: Inherently safer design initiatives
controls.barrier_role: Reduce or eliminate hazards through design rather than relying on controls
controls.lod_basis: Highest order of hazard control through elimination or substitution at design stage

supporting_text: initiatives typically associated with the most effective and preventive efforts found in the hierarchy of controls, inherently safer design, engineering solutions

controls.name: Audit practices for nitrous oxide operations
controls.barrier_role: Verify and assure effectiveness of safety controls and compliance with procedures
controls.lod_basis: Independent verification layer to confirm barriers are functioning

supporting_text: connected to ongoing engineering assessments, audits, and training for operations personnel

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
